title: CLI, Configuration, Packaging, Scheduling, Overlap, Logs, and Recovery · Python Build Room
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds installable and operable crawler release candidate from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of installable and operable crawler release candidate while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chan…
objectives: Design typed CLI and configuration precedence for targets, policy, budgets, output, dry-run, checkpoint, resume, revision, and secret references.
Build an import-safe package with declared dependencies, console entry point, resources, metadata, license, and reproducible artifact identity.
Install the immutable artifact into a clean environment and run help, fixture crawl, controlled local crawl, output validation, and clean shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Cli Configuration
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 6121 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate CLI and package contracts, clean installs, scheduler overlap control, retention, restore, and operational ownership; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Cli Configuration.

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-operations-retention-recovery-1adfx4
# Competency: scraper-operations-retention-recovery.
# Case 6409: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly.
def evidence_scraper_operations_retention_recovery_1adfx4(target_platforms: int = 3, clean_installs: int = 3, scheduled_runs: int = 12, overlap_incidents: int = 0, restore_minutes: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1adfx4 = "1adfx4-4xfda1-6409"
    assert evidence_variant_1adfx4
    evidence_axes_1adfx4 = (
        "authorization1adfx4",
        "url6409",
        "response4xfda1",
        "tree1adfx46409",
        "frontier64091adfx4",
        "report4xfda16409",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1adfx4)) == 6
    if target_platforms < 1 or clean_installs < 0:
        return False, clean_installs, "package-matrix"
    if clean_installs != target_platforms:
        return False, clean_installs, "clean-install-gap"
    if scheduled_runs < 1 or overlap_incidents > 0:
        return False, overlap_incidents, "schedule-overlap"
    if restore_minutes < 0 or restore_minutes > 20:
        return False, restore_minutes, "restore-budget"
    assert clean_installs == target_platforms and overlap_incidents == 0
    return True, clean_installs, "operable-crawler-release"
